output_text:
S: 19-year-old woman presents with iron deficiency anemia. She denies fevers, chills, night sweats, lymphadenopathy, nausea, vomiting, or change in bowel or bladder habits. She specifically denies melena or hematochezia. She has a history of having a baby 5 months ago and currently uses an IUD.
O: Bone marrow biopsy on 07/21/10 showed normal cellular marrow with trilineage hematopoiesis. On 07/22/10, hemoglobin was 6.5, and she was transfused 2 units of packed red blood cells. Iron levels were 5 and percent transferrin was 2. There was no evidence of hemolysis. Physical exam findings are not detailed beyond the general note.
A: Iron deficiency anemia.
P: Continue iron supplements and Levaquin.
label: gemma4.E4B.mtp.smoke
